senses.definition: to interpret something in a different or new way
examples: The documentary reinterprets the events of the war to highlight overlooked perspectives.
Her lecture reinterprets classic theories in light of new evidence.

senses.definition: to bury again in a grave or tomb
examples: After the initial burial, the body was reinterred in a family tomb.
The remains were reinterred in a new location with respectful ceremony.

senses.definition: the act of burying again; the process of reburial
examples: The crew is reinterring the remains after securing legal permissions.
Reinterring occurred at dawn in the presence of family members.

senses.definition: to question someone again, often in a more detailed or persistent way
examples: The detective decided to reinterrogate the suspect after new evidence emerged.
Officials reinterrogate witnesses to clarify conflicting statements.

senses.definition: interrogated again; questioned again, usually in a more detailed or persistent way
examples: The suspect was reinterrogated to verify earlier statements.
Officials reinterrogated the witness after new footage surfaced.

senses.definition: questions someone again, often in a more detailed or persistent way
examples: The officers reinterrogates the witness to confirm the timeline.
New evidence causes investigators to reinterrogates several suspects.

senses.definition: the act of questioning someone again in a detailed or persistent way
examples: They are reinterrogating the key witness tonight.
Officials are reinterrogating several suspects.

senses.definition: the act of questioning someone again, often in a more detailed or persistent way
examples: The reinterrogation of the suspect lasted several hours.
There was debate about the appropriateness of the reinterrogation.

senses.definition: the acts of questioning someone again; multiple instances of reinterrogation
examples: The file includes several reinterrogations by different investigators.
Ethical concerns arose from the series of reinterrogations.

senses.definition: to interrupt again; to cut off an ongoing action or speech again
examples: Her speech was reinterrupt when the door opened.
The meeting was reinterrupt by a loud noise outside.

senses.definition: the act of interrupting again; a second or repeated interruption
examples: There was a reinterruption during the presentation.
The reinterruption disrupted the flow of the discussion.

senses.definition: plural form; likely referring to people or things that re-enter or reinsert, context dependent
examples: The reinters of the plan faced logistical challenges.
Various reinters contributed to the project.

senses.definition: to intervene again; to become involved in a situation to influence an outcome once more
examples: The mediator decided to reintervene to break the deadlock.
Societal pressure forced authorities to reintervene in the dispute.

senses.definition: involved again to influence an outcome or situation
examples: Officials reintervened after the new data was released.
The committee reintervened to steer the negotiations.

senses.definition: intervening again in a situation
examples: Several parties are reintervening to shape the outcome.
The advisor is reintervening to mediate the talks.

senses.definition: the act of intervening again; an instance of reintervention
examples: The reintervention shifted the negotiation dynamics.
There was a call for careful reintervention by all parties.

senses.definition: to interview someone again; questions are asked anew
examples: They decided to reinterview the witness for clarification.
Journalists reinterviewed the official after new documents appeared.

senses.definition: interviewed someone again
examples: The witness was reinterviewed after the new footage was found.
Experts reinterviewed the leading scientists.

senses.definition: the act of interviewing someone again
examples: Officials are reinterviewing witnesses to verify details.
A new round of reinterviewing is planned.

senses.definition: the act of interviewing someone again
examples: Several reinterviews were conducted during the trial.
Reinterviews helped clarify inconsistencies.

senses.definition: to restore someone to a throne or position of power
examples: The prince was reinthroned after the revolution.
The monarchy was reinthroned with ceremonial grandeur.

senses.definition: to make intimate again; to restore a close relationship or familiarity
examples: They worked to reintimate their friendship after years apart.
The counselor helped reintimate trust between the partners.

senses.definition: the act of making intimate again; renewed closeness or familiarity
examples: Reintimation of their bond surprised the group.
The therapy focused on reintimation and communication.

senses.definition: to rename or re-titularize; a rare or misspelled form of re-titile
examples: The group debated whether to reintitule the project.
Some documents attempted to reintitule the grant.

senses.definition: to strengthen or secure again, especially a defense or position
examples: The government aims to reintrench its borders.
Efforts were made to reintrench funding for the program.
